title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a guide giving information to visitors at a town museum.
audio_script: Speaker: Welcome to the Old Town Museum. My name is Rachel, and I'll explain what you can see today. The museum is small, but there is a lot to discover, especially if you are interested in how people lived in this town a hundred years ago. We are standing in the entrance hall now. Please start your visit on the first floor, where you will find a display about [14]local shops[/14]. It includes old photographs, signs and even a shop counter from 1910. After that, go upstairs to the family rooms. The most popular object there is a child's [15]wooden horse[/15], which was found in a house near the market. Please do not touch it, because it is very old. At half past eleven, there will be a short film in the education room. It lasts [16]fifteen[/16] minutes and tells the story of the town fire. There are information cards in each room, but if you prefer, you can borrow an audio guide from reception. Please keep your ticket with you, because staff may ask to see it when you go upstairs. If you want a drink, the café is open today, but only until [17]three[/17]. It serves cakes, sandwiches and hot chocolate. The museum shop is beside the exit. This month, the best souvenir is a set of [18]postcards[/18] showing old street views. They are not expensive and are easy to post. Finally, remember that the museum is closed next Monday because staff will be moving some paintings. It will open again on [19]Wednesday[/19] morning at the usual time.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the first floor has a display about local shops.
The most popular object is a child's wooden horse.
The speaker says the short film lasts fifteen minutes.
The café is open only until three.
The speaker says the best souvenir this month is a set of postcards.
The museum will open again on Wednesday morning.
